page: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128gb is a memory-optimized server instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this configuration operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with 16 physical cores and a single thread per core allocation. The server is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, indicating that workloads requiring substantial storage capacity must rely on external network storage solutions. There are no onboard GPUs.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core scaling. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the instance achieves top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% of tested servers. This single-threaded strength is mirrored in data compression tasks, where gzip compression and bzip3 decompression both rank in the top 10%. However, multi-core performance in stress-ng and bzip3 decompression falls into the average category. Memory performance is robust, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 25%, while uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain in the middle 50% tier.

Given its shared CPU allocation and high memory density, this instance offers a cost-effective profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated multi-core CPU utilization. The high RAM capacity relative to vCPUs makes it suitable for in-memory caching, database operations, and memory-heavy web serving. However, the shared CPU model and average multi-core benchmark results represent a tradeoff for sustained parallel computing tasks. It is best suited for Redis deployments, medium-sized database hosting, and memory-bound general-purpose applications.
description: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128gb is a memory-optimized server instance from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor on the x86_64 architecture, it operates at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The instance lacks a dedicated GPU and relies on a 1 GB network-attached local storage volume. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth align with average tiers. PassMark memory testing ranks in the top 25%. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand sustained, dedicated multi-core processing. It is well-suited for in-memory databases like Redis, caching layers, and memory-intensive web serving.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128gb is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and network storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128gb is a memory-optimized server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, ideal for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high single-core performance and dense memory allocation for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 16 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
1 GB network-attached local storage with no local GPU
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-heavy web serving
